Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Issue Orders
Off Calendar Dec-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Shelf for Edith Williams (Petitioner).
Jim Efting for David Clarke.

Counsel indicated that they are not aware of anybody objecting. Mr. Efting provided a revised order and requested that the Court set a date for the Special Administrator to file his accounting and a date for the new Administrator to file her accounting. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-25-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jim Efting and Katherine Efting for David Clarke (Beneficiary).
Jim Efting specially appearing for Wendy Krog on behalf of San Francisco SPCA.
Amy Shelf for Edith Williams (Petitioner in the 2016 Will).

The Court noted that before it are two competing petitions for probate, stated that it is glad to see that the parties reached resolution and asked why a petition for approval of the settlement can't be by way of a fully noticed petition. Mr. Efting explained that they were seeking to do an ex parte petition because they are changing the administrator from a professional to Edith, that they want to get it moving, expressed concern about a fully noticed petition being set very far out and stated that he has no problem with one way or the other. The Court informed Counsel that petitions are being calendared about 30-days out. Both Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to December 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-25-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Continued To Oct-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Shelf for and with Edith Williams.
Wendy Krog for San Francisco SPCA.
Jim Efting and Katherine Efting for David Clarke (Beneficiary).
David Rose for DeSales Catholic High School and St. Francis Catholic Church.

Mr. Efting stated that Mr. Clarke and the Attorney who prepared the 2016 estate plan were deposed, that he expects to get discovery back shortly, that discovery should be complete after that, that mediation has been discussed and that his preference is to have trial set in January. Ms. Krog said yes to mediation and joins in on the request to set trial in January. Mr. Rose stated that he agrees to participate in mediation, and that he agrees discovery is practically completed, but he would like to conduct discovery regarding medical records. The parties elected to do private mediation. Both Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to October 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a further status conference with the understanding that the parties will have all discovery done and mediation completed. By October 17th, the parties are to submit a joint status statement reporting results on where they stand and they are to meet and confer on the length of trial and what the trial is going to look like.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Continued To Oct-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Directing Payment Of Attorney Fees Costs
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Shelf for petitioner Edith Williams; David Rose for St. Francis Catholic Church and DeSales High School; Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges, proposed special administrator.

The Court observed that the matter to be determined through today's hearing was not the right to an award of attorney's fees, but only the amount to be awarded. Mr. Rose stated that there was a conflict of interest because of a prior relationship between petitioner Williams and attorney Amy Shelf, and that no award of attorneys fees should be made as the motion by the objectors had some merit. Ms. Shelf responded that the Probate Court had made a specific finding that the motion by objectors was frivolous, and that Ms. Shelf was entitled to fees and costs upon a proper showing. She noted that no objection had been raised to any item of fees or costs set forth in the petition. The Court stated that the only issue remaining in dispute is the request for additional fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar Jun-01-2017 Continued To Jun-22-17 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 202

Petition For Order Directing Payment Of Attorney Fees Costs
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Continued
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Shelf for and with Edith Williams.
Wendy Krog for San Francisco SPCA.
James Efting and Katherine Efting for David Clarke (Beneficiary).
David Rose for DeSales Catholic High School and St. Francis Catholic Church.

Ms. Shelf stated that Mr. Rose has made several attempts at discovery, that his Clients don't have standing until the 1983 Will is proven, and that the Charities have received several notes from the Examiners and nothing was done to cure them. Ms. Krog stated that none of the Charities are Beneficiaries in the 2016 Will. Mr. Rose stated that he made attempts to cure the deficiency and that a supplement was filed yesterday addressing the notes. Mr. Efting expressed concern about no one watching over the property and raised the issue of a Special Administrator. Both Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to July 19, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encourages the Parties to talk to each other about how to proceed, having a Special Administrator and if useful, mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
